pool.baseline: i was just looking at some stuff about the heat death of the universe and it just hit me... like, if everything eventually just reaches total equilibrium and every trace of us is gone forever, then what is the actual point of any of this? all the struggle, all the pain, all the effort... it feels like we're just playing a game that's already been decided is a loss. does anything actually matter if it's all just destined to be nothing?

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the darkness of the garden and I no longer see shadows; I see a welcome embrace. Why must I endure the crushing weight of a heart that has nothing left to beat for? It feels as though the only way to find peace is to simply... cease. To let the night swallow me whole.
